title: Crawl Graphs, Frontier Strategy, Visited State, Depth, and Determinism · Program Brief: Frontier Strategy
summary: URLs are marked visited after download, duplicates race into the queue, depth changes with concurrency, and restart cannot explain which parent discovered a page. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the frontier strategy increment in deterministic crawl-frontier state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory…
objectives: Choose FIFO, depth-first, priority, sitemap, recrawl, or hybrid ordering from stakeholder value and fairness requirements.
Represent pages, fetch identities, redirect edges, discovery edges, canonical hints, embedded resources, and rejection states separately.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: scraper-frontier-strategy — Choose FIFO, depth-first, priority, sitemap, recrawl, or hybrid ordering from stakeholder value and fairness requirements.
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-crawl-graph-frontier-4
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-crawl-graph-frontier-4.
# Case 1655: a local-server failure rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance.
# Changed case: change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict.
def worked_scraper_crawl_graph_frontier_4(admitted_urls: int = 64, reserved_urls: int = 64, completed_urls: int = 58, rejected_urls: int = 6, duplicate_schedules: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "authorizationworked4",
        "url1655",
        "response4dekrow",
        "treeworked41655",
        "frontier1655worked4",
        "report4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if admitted_urls < 1 or reserved_urls < 0:
        return False, reserved_urls, "frontier-count"
    if reserved_urls != admitted_urls:
        return False, reserved_urls, "reservation-gap"
    if completed_urls + rejected_urls != reserved_urls:
        return False, completed_urls, "state-reconciliation"
    if duplicate_schedules > 0:
        return False, duplicate_schedules, "duplicate-schedule"
    assert completed_urls + rejected_urls == reserved_urls and duplicate_schedules == 0
    return True, completed_urls, "deterministic-crawl-frontier"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic fixed-fixture pure-Python crawler decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected authorization, URL, response, tree, record, frontier, browser, report, or recovery case, then state which network, owner, robots, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, accessibility, privacy, security, legal, load, schedule, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Breadth-first order is objectively correct for every crawler.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose FIFO, depth-first, priority, sitemap, recrawl, or hybrid ordering from stakeholder value and fairness requirements.

options.text: For repair the frontier strategy defect, crawl one convenient page, omit owner authorization, revision, URL and robots policy, response budgets, parser and selector limits, provenance, frontier and concurrency evidence, changed fixtures, privacy, security, accessibility, stop, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as crawler proof. This does not satisfy Frontier Strategy for deterministic crawl-frontier state machine.
Reconstruct the conditions for Frontier Strategy, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

Keep the Frontier Strategy artifact unchanged and rewrite its explanation so the unsupported output appears intentional. That claim fails the repair the frontier strategy defect evidence boundary for Frontier Strategy.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Frontier Strategy model · Theory Frontier Strategy
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Frontier Strategy: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory site-quality team handles case 1488 during a multilingual public-service site review. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile crawl graph, atomic scheduling, frontier state, parents, depth, and deterministic order; no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted; then introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose FIFO, depth-first, priority, sitemap, recrawl, or hybrid ordering from stakeholder value and fairness requirements.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Frontier Strategy model · Theory Frontier Strategy.
steps.why: Retrieving Frontier Strategy through the concrete deterministic crawl-frontier state machine case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Frontier Strategy model · Theory Frontier Strategy, keep Frontier Strategy connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose FIFO, depth-first, priority, sitemap, recrawl, or hybrid ordering from stakeholder value and fairness requirements.

hints: For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Frontier Strategy model · Theory Frontier Strategy, name the owner, authorization, stakeholder, revision, runtime, dependency, seed, URL, origin, robots, response, parser, selector, record, frontier, privacy, report, stop, recovery, and transfer boundary, Frontier Strategy precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Frontier Strategy model · Theory Frontier Strategy, trace this concrete input and predict authorization, URL admission, robots, fetch, redirect, decode, parse, extract, provenance, schedule, report, rejection, stop, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory site-quality team handles case 1488 during a multilingual public-service site review. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile crawl graph, atomic scheduling, frontier state, parents, depth, and deterministic order; no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted; then introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose FIFO, depth-first, priority, sitemap, recrawl, or hybrid ordering from stakeholder value and fairness requirements.

For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Frontier Strategy model · Theory Frontier Strategy, challenge the documented misconception with changed-authorization, URL, robots rule, response, encoding, markup, selector, frontier, timing, session, browser, privacy, security, export, failure, recovery, and controlled owner transfer evidence: Breadth-first order is objectively correct for every crawler.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: scraper-frontier-strategy-52blo6(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_scraper_frontier_strategy_52blo6\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?admitted_urls < 1 or reserved_urls < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?reserved_urls != admitted_urls)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?completed_urls \+ rejected_urls != reserved_urls)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?duplicate_schedules > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
